run.root_cause: The notebook-derived Bayesian prior + embedding probe pipeline is live; next steps are submission bundle parity and calibration.

plan.reason: All positive priors converge on a single directive: ship the raw-probe submission bundle immediately with no prior fusion and no calibration. The adopt-now cards (confidence 0.68) prioritize submission parity above all else. The negative prior (avoid @ 0.58) explicitly vetoes any calibration or class-coverage work before the leaderboard submission. The decision stage returned verdict "submission-required" with decision_type "branch_submit" and portfolio_intent "anchor_baseline". This is an explicit submission-packaging step, not an experiment iteration.
plan.title: Raw probe baseline submission bundle
plan.hypothesis: Packaging run-0001-perch-baseline as a raw-probe submission bundle with no prior fusion and no calibration will establish the first leaderboard anchor at approximately val ROC-AUC 0.665, grounding all future calibration and coverage-expansion decisions.

problem_frame.title: Raw probe baseline submission bundle
problem_frame.root_cause: The Perch cached-probe baseline (val ROC-AUC 0.665) is locally validated but has no leaderboard anchor. The critical blocker is submission bundle parity — without a Kaggle score, all calibration, class-coverage expansion, and prior-fusion tuning are ungrounded. The 0.327 gap between training soundscape (0.992) and validation soundscape (0.665) ROC-AUC confirms domain shift/overfitting, but addressing it before establishing a leaderboard baseline is premature.
problem_frame.hypothesis: All positive priors converge on a single directive: ship the raw-probe submission bundle immediately with no prior fusion and no calibration. The adopt-now card (confidence 0.68) and root-cause card (confidence 0.68) both prioritize submission parity above all else. The require-tier cards (confidence 0.88) establish that holdout validation is the iteration signal and that embedding heads retain future value. The negative prior (avoid @ 0.58) explicitly vetoes any calibration or class-coverage work before the leaderboard submission. The conditional leads (class-coverage deficit 23/75 classes) are correctly gated on a post-submission leaderboard anchor. Collapsing to a single calibration sweep now would violate the veto policy and waste cycles on ungrounded tuning. The right decision is to push the raw probe baseline through the submission pipeline, then use the leaderboard delta to inform a multi-branch plan targeting the highest-value axes: class-coverage expansion and domain-shift diagnosis.
adopt_now: [card:research-run-0001-perch-baseline-md-adopt-now-guidance] Ship raw-probe bundle first with no prior fusion and no calibration tweaks. The 0.665 local score is the honest baseline; a Kaggle score anchors every subsequent decision.
[card:01-validated-findings-md-validation-and-metric-semantics] Use holdout validation ROC-AUC as the primary local signal for iteration, not training soundscape metrics.
[card:01-validated-findings-md-perch-and-embedding-findings] Retain embedding-head style models as they can add complementary signal even when standalone scores are not best.
consider: [card:research-run-0002-0001-perch-probe-iteration-class-coverage-verification-and-calibration-md-run-0002-0001-perch-probe-iteration-class-coverage-verification-and-calibration] After leaderboard anchor is established, investigate the class coverage deficit: model fitted 52/75 classes (31% uncovered). This is likely the structural bottleneck for score improvement, but must be deferred until raw baseline is submitted.

[card:research-run-0001-perch-baseline-md-root-cause] Post-submission, use leaderboard delta to diagnose whether the 0.992→0.665 training-validation gap is primarily domain shift or class-coverage driven, then calibrate prior fusion accordingly.
reject: [card:research-run-0001-perch-baseline-md-reject] Any calibration, class-coverage expansion, or prior-fusion tuning before the raw-probe leaderboard submission. These are wasted cycles without a grounded Kaggle score.
Attempting to close the 0.327 training-validation ROC-AUC gap via model architecture changes before confirming the leaderboard parity of the current probe head.

candidate_branches.title: Class coverage expansion probe
candidate_branches.hypothesis: Expanding probe head from 52 to 75 classes recovers signal from 23 uncovered classes
candidate_branches.rationale: Conditional lead identifies 31% class coverage deficit as structural bottleneck

candidate_branches.title: Calibrated prior fusion v2
candidate_branches.hypothesis: Properly calibrated Bayesian prior fusion adds lift once tuned against a grounded leaderboard score
candidate_branches.rationale: Prior fusion currently adds negligible signal; leaderboard anchor enables grounded calibration

candidate_branches.title: Domain shift mitigation soundscape augmentation
candidate_branches.hypothesis: Augmenting training data with validation-distribution-like transformations reduces the 0.327 train-val ROC-AUC gap
candidate_branches.rationale: Training 0.992 vs validation 0.665 suggests domain shift in soundscape characteristics
